lawFacts.CA: California requires a 10-day waiting period for all firearm purchases, regardless of whether you already own firearms.
lawFacts.TX: Texas is a permitless carry state — any non-prohibited resident 21 or older may carry a handgun without a license.
lawFacts.FL: Florida became a permitless carry state in July 2023; the optional CWL remains useful for interstate reciprocity.
lawFacts.CO: Colorado requires background checks for all firearm transfers, including private sales conducted without an FFL.
lawFacts.IL: Illinois requires a Firearm Owner's Identification (FOID) card to legally possess or purchase any firearm or ammunition.
lawFacts.NY: Following Bruen, New York's CCIA sensitive-places restrictions are being litigated — several provisions remain enjoined pending federal court review.
lawFacts.CT: Connecticut has a 14-day waiting period for all firearm purchases and bans assault weapons and large-capacity magazines.
lawFacts.HI: Hawaii requires a permit to purchase any firearm and mandates registration of all firearms within 5 days of acquisition.
lawFacts.AZ: Arizona is a permitless carry state with no firearm registration requirement and strong statewide preemption laws.
lawFacts.GA: Georgia's permitless carry law has been in effect since April 2022; the optional Weapons Carry License still provides reciprocity in many states.
lawFacts.OH: Ohio became a permitless carry state in June 2022; carriers must inform law enforcement of a firearm only if directly asked.
lawFacts.VA: Virginia requires universal background checks for all firearm sales and has a one-handgun-per-month purchase limit.
lawFacts.WA: Washington has a 10-day waiting period for all firearm purchases and bans magazines capable of holding more than 10 rounds.
lawFacts.TN: Tennessee has been a permitless carry state since July 2021; the optional Handgun Carry Permit remains valuable for reciprocity.
lawFacts.NC: North Carolina repealed its Pistol Purchase Permit system in March 2023 — buyers now complete a standard NICS background check at an FFL.
lawFacts.NJ: New Jersey requires a Firearms Purchaser ID Card for long guns and a separate permit for each handgun purchase.
lawFacts.FED: The Supreme Court's 2022 Bruen decision established that firearms regulations must be consistent with the nation's historical tradition — reshaping how courts evaluate 2A challenges.
